label: fix129-create-work-order-contract
prompt: Read /home/mfspx/LUCIDOTA/06_SCHEMA/041_boring_beast_loop_contracts.sql which defines lucidota_control.work_order_contract with column work_order_key. Also read /home/mfspx/LUCIDOTA/06_SCHEMA/work_order.schema.json. The table was never applied to the DB. Write /home/mfspx/LUCIDOTA/06_SCHEMA/134_apply_work_order_contract.sql that: 1) Re-runs the CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS for work_order_contract exactly as in 041 (idempotent), 2) Then ALTER TABLE lucidota_control.work_order_contract RENAME COLUMN work_order_key TO work_order_id IF the schema.json requires work_order_id (check the json), 3) Adds IF NOT EXISTS status TEXT and lane TEXT columns if schema requires them. Write only valid idempotent SQL. Add clear comments explaining why each step is needed. No DDL that isn't backed by what you read.

label: fix130-absurd-job-state-note
prompt: Rewrite /home/mfspx/LUCIDOTA/06_SCHEMA/130_fix_absurd_job_status_enum.sql with this correction: The original migration incorrectly tried to rename absurd_queue_job.status values from 'succeeded' to 'COMPLETED'. These are different schemas: absurd_queue_job uses a Postgres enum (queued/running/succeeded/failed/dead_lettered/cancelled) while absurd_job.schema.json defines a JSON envelope abstraction (CREATED/RUNNING/COMPLETED/FAILED/DEAD_LETTER) — they are NOT the same object. The only safe change was ADD VALUE 'COMPLETED' to the enum (already applied). Write a corrected SQL file that: 1) Has a comment block explaining this distinction, 2) Confirms the ADD VALUE IF NOT EXISTS (idempotent), 3) Has a commented-out UPDATE block with a warning: DO NOT RUN — breaks live workers polling status=succeeded. Output only the SQL file content.

label: fix132-simplemem-status-column
prompt: Read /home/mfspx/LUCIDOTA/06_SCHEMA/053_simplemem_candidate_index.sql and /home/mfspx/LUCIDOTA/06_SCHEMA/047_simplemem_eval_promotion_bridge.sql. The table lucidota_control.simplemem_candidate currently has NO status column (confirmed in live DB). Migration 132 tried to add a CHECK constraint on a nonexistent column and failed. Write /home/mfspx/LUCIDOTA/06_SCHEMA/135_simplemem_candidate_status.sql that: 1) ALTER TABLE lucidota_control.simplemem_candidate ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS status TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT 'CANDIDATE', 2) ADD CONSTRAINT IF NOT EXISTS chk_simplemem_status CHECK (status IN ('CANDIDATE','ACCEPTED','REJECTED')). Both idempotent. Comment explaining the column was missing from original schema. Valid SQL only.
